other_observers: Cheri Orwig, Larry Griffin; Josh Bruenning first found the bird earlier in the day. It was around for 3 or 4 days and seen by many observers.
specific_location: At the east end of Fossil Creek Reservoir
description: Rufous head with a yellow forehead. It had gray spots on a rufous side. It had a mostly gray back with a dark tail. It also had rufous to pink breast. It has a small blue bill with a black tip. White on the lower flanks.Behaviors: Swimming with American Wigeon and Northern ShovelerCall: Didn’t hear itPlumage: Adult Molting from Basic to Alternate
photographer: Photographer: Cole Wild, Cole Wild, Cole Wild
